fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Virginia Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity virginia, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to virginia, speech-language pathologist compact virginia, virginia speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.mailing_address: Virginia Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology, Perimeter Center, 9960 Mayland Drive, Suite 300, Henrico, VA 23233-1463

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Virginia.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register on the Virginia Department of Health Professions Online Services portal and complete the Speech-Language Pathologist licensure application. Pay the non-refundable application fee of $130.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Have official transcripts, verifying completion of a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology from an accredited program, sent directly from your academic institution to the Board office.

steps.title: Verify Supervised Post-Graduate Professional Experience
steps.description: Ensure your supervisor submits the 'Verification of Supervised Post-Graduate Professional Experience' form directly to the Board, documenting successful completion of your clinical fellowship (CFY) or equivalent.

steps.title: Pass National Examination (Praxis)
steps.description: Arrange for official scores from the Praxis exam in Speech-Language Pathology (ETS code R7119 for Virginia) to be sent directly from Educational Testing Service (ETS) to the Board.

steps.title: Pass Virginia State Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Complete the online open-book Virginia State Jurisprudence Examination. Instructions for accessing and completing this exam are available on the Board's website or within the online application.

documents.detail: Virginia State Police and FBI background check.

telepractice_text: Virginia permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Virginia issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Virginia is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Virginia SLP license cost?
Is Virginia part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Virginia allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Virginia have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Virginia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Virginia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Virginia is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
